preamble: The voiced postalveolar affricate [dʒ] shifts to the voiceless alveolar affricate [ts], involving both devoicing and place change from postalveolar to alveolar. This complex sound change is attested in historical sound change and phonological variation, particularly in language contact and borrowing situations.

examples.note: Historical shift combining devoicing and place retraction from postalveolar to alveolar in Proto-Slavic evolution.
Adaptation of English [dʒ] to Polish [ts] in loanword phonological integration, reflecting native phonological inventory.
Allophonic realization in rapid or casual speech where devoicing and place change occur together.
